title: [Verif] Add FoldAssume Pass
body:
This adds a new verif pass called `fold-assume`, which folds assumptions into the enable signal of other assertions on a per block granularity.

**Motivation**: I noticed recently when running some benchmarks on btormc and patronus (BMC using bitwuzla) that both underlying solvers are absolutely garbage at handling assumptions, e.g. a design with no assumptions which took 5s to find UNSAT (for k=500), then took 35s to find the same result when I changed the assertions to assumptions. Basically, we need a way to support assumptions without incurring the overhead of smt `constraint` ops. 

This pass thus implements assumptions as enable signals for assertions, and currently only does so for assertlikes that have `i1` properties (until I can convince myself that doing so for `ltl` properties doesn't break semantics or to add what allows us to preserve semantics in that case). 

This follows how assumptions tend to work in modern verification tools, you accumulate a knowledge base of assumed properties and then use those as the antecedent of any subsequent assertion, which in our case is equivalent to folding our knowledge base into the enable signal of assertions. 

To simplify things, I make the assumption that the `--combine-assert-like` pass is run beforehand to reduce duplicated work between passes.
